entry.title: Poor Man's Polaroid
entry.quote: This is an instant camera that uses thermal printer to print photos, the same one that prints your receipts at the store. Photos aren't the same quality as the self developing film that polaroid uses, but they do have some, uhh, charm to them, I suppose.

entry.title: Modern Illustration
entry.quote: An archive of illustration from c.1950-1975, shining a spotlight on pioneering illustrators and their work.

entry.title: Cleaning out his mom's house, Boston man finds his ancestor's freedom papers
entry.quote: After clearing out his mother's home, Aaron Haynes discovered his ancestor's freedom papers and began the journey of working to uncover more about the document and the man whose freedom it guaranteed.

entry.title: Lock Scroll With a Vengeance
entry.quote: I just dated myself by reminiscing Lotus 1-2-3, I’m curious how it feels to others

entry.title: The Government Uses Targeted Advertising to Track Your Location. Here's What We Need to Do.
entry.quote: We've all had the unsettling experience of seeing an ad online that reveals just how much advertisers know about our lives. You're right to be disturbed. Those very same online ad systems have been used by the government to warrantlessly track peoples' locations, new reporting has confirmed.
